mission.title: Install and Configure Kitops on Kubernetes
mission.description: KitOps is an open source DevOps tool for packaging and versioning AI/ML models, datasets, code, and configuration into an OCI Artifact. Installing KitOps allows you to manage your AI/ML projects efficiently and securely, leveraging the power of the CNCF ecosystem.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for KitOps
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for KitOps to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply the KitOps Manifests
steps.description: Download and apply the necessary manifests from the KitOps GitHub repository.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the KitOps pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KitOps will have the KitOps pods running in the 'kitops' namespace, and you will be able to access the KitOps UI at http://localhost:8080. Resource limits will be configured as specified.

uninstall.title: Remove KitOps Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall KitOps, delete the resources you applied earlier and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KitOps
upgrade.description: To upgrade KitOps, apply the new version of the manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If a pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kitops`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas Exceeded
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource quota errors, ensure that your cluster has enough resources allocated for the KitOps components.

troubleshooting.title: Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot find KitOps resources, ensure that you are querying the correct namespace with `-n kitops`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
